questions.question: What new model architecture is proposed in the document?
evidence_snippets: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms.

questions.question: How many identical layers does the Transformer encoder have?
questions.gold_answer: The encoder has six identical layers.
evidence_snippets: The encoder is composed of a stack of N = 6 identical layers.

questions.question: What is the BLEU score achieved by the Transformer on the English-to-German translation task?
evidence_snippets: Our model achieves 28.4 BLEU on the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task.

questions.question: How does the training cost of the Transformer (big) model compare to that of the previous state-of-the-art model on the English-to-French task?
questions.gold_answer: The Transformer (big) model has less than 1/4 the training cost of the previous state-of-the-art model.
evidence_snippets: outperforming all of the previously published single models, at less than 1/4 the training cost of the previous state-of-the-art model.

questions.question: What is the dimensionality of input and output in the position-wise feed-forward networks of the Transformer?
questions.gold_answer: dmodel = 512, and the inner-layer has dimensionality df f = 2048.
evidence_snippets: the dimensionality of input and output is dmodel = 512 , and the inner-layer has dimensionality df f = 2048.

questions.question: Why does the Transformer model use a sinusoidal function for positional encodings?
questions.gold_answer: It was hypothesized that the sinusoidal function would allow the model to easily learn to attend by relative positions.
evidence_snippets: We chose this function because we hypothesized it would allow the model to easily learn to attend by relative positions.

questions.question: Summarize the main advantage of using self-attention compared to recurrent networks as discussed in the document.
questions.gold_answer: Self-attention layers allow for constant computational complexity and parallelization, making it faster and more efficient for sequence transduction tasks.
evidence_snippets: A self-attention layer connects all positions with a constant number of sequentially executed operations
self-attention layers are faster than recurrent layers.

questions.question: Referencing Table 2, how does the BLEU score of the Transformer (big) compare to the BLEU scores of other models on the English-to-French task?
questions.gold_answer: The Transformer (big) achieves the highest BLEU score of 41.8 among the compared models.
evidence_snippets: outperforming all of the previously published single models.

questions.question: What are the advantages of the Transformer architecture over convolutional models as detailed in Table 1?
questions.gold_answer: The Transformer allows for constant path lengths in processing, while convolutions increase the length of paths required to connect input and output positions.
evidence_snippets: A single convolutional layer ... increases the length of the longest paths.

questions.question: Discuss how the choice of using 8 parallel attention heads improves the performance of the Transformer.
questions.gold_answer: It allows the model to jointly attend to information from different representation subspaces at different positions, enhancing the learning capacity.
evidence_snippets: Multi-head attention allows the model to jointly attend to information from different representation subspaces.
